page_title: Using AI in your business? Here's what to think about first - Mandata
meta_description: Discover the risks of using Artificial Intelligence (AI) in your business and how to protect your data, and how Mandata is AI responsible.

text: 1) Public AI tools aren’t private Many save and reuse what you type. That could mean your business or customer data is stored or even used in future responses. 2) Hosting matters Some tools are hosted outside the UK/EU, which can create GDPR and data protection concerns, especially when handling personal or sensitive data. 3) Not all tools are secure Open-source or public AI tools can introduce security issues, inaccuracies, and even copyright risks. Ensure that suitable due diligence is carried out when selecting new technology partners or AI tools. 4) Bias and misinformation exist AI is only as good as the data it’s trained on — and that data often includes internet bias and stereotypes. We’ve been evaluating AI through the lens of security, privacy, and transparency Your data is never shared with public AI tools We’re designing features with GDPR compliance at the core Security, transparency, and trust guide every decision we make Don’t input private or sensitive info Ask your providers how your data is handled Check where the tools are hosted Always review and fact-check AI output Create an internal AI policy for your team Want to know more about how Mandata is integrating AI securely? Get in touch. 🔗 You can also find out more regarding the safety and best practice of AI via the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO): ico.org.uk oic.ie

text: Stateline Logistics Group has been a trusted name in Irish logistics for more than three decades. Based in Santry, County Dublin, the business employs over 85 people, runs a fleet of more than 55 vehicles, and serves more than 110 customers across the country. Over the past two years, Stateline has experienced remarkable growth, doubling in size. The company attributes this success to two clear priorities: delivering a premium, reliable service for its customers and putting driver welfare at the centre of its operations. With a workforce made up largely of drivers, the business has built a reputation for looking after its people, knowing that they are absolutely crucial to its business model. Stateline offers a diverse range of services, including container transport, supermarket deliveries, curtainsider trailers, hazardous goods, waste jobs, container devanning, and storage. This variety keeps the business sharp and ensures its drivers remain some of the most adaptable in the industry. Eureka Supporting Business Growth For Over A Decade Now led by Managing Director Ryan Brady, Stateline recognised that outdated systems were holding back their operations. This prompted a search for a suitable Transport Management System (TMS). Having worked his way through every level of the business, from driving and planning to eventually leading the business, Ryan saw firsthand the need for a TMS, to boost efficiency and support their ambitious growth plans Before MoveIT, it was a mix of spreadsheets, phone calls, notepads and a lot of people relying on memory recalls Ryan , “ It worked to a point, but it wasn’t sustainable if we wanted to scale up Communication gaps, missed opportunities, and too much time spent on admin. A lot of information was in people’s heads or written on a piece of paper, we were probably Tipp-Ex’s biggest customers At the time, moving away from paper-based processes and spreadsheets required some internal persuasion, but once the systems were in place, the benefits were immediately clear. Fast forward 10 years, and the team at Stateline is still seeing the benefits of Eureka. We have now been with Eureka since 2015. It took a while to convince the father at the time, but once we got the system in, it’s been a game changer Today, the company uses Eureka’s MoveIT, MaintainIT, and a number of integrated apps, including Telegram. Each solution fits neatly into the way the team operates, making day-to-day work smoother and more efficient. The team love it. It’s not overly complicated, and it actually works in practice says Ryan . “ Eureka won’t sell you something that doesn’t work– which is rare with software in our industry Stateline has achieved an impressive 97% revenue growth over the past two years, with Ryan attributing much of this success to the support provided by the Eureka MoveIT TMS. Eureka solving daily operational challenges Since adopting Eureka, the team now has full visibility of operations in real-time, and communication between office staff and drivers has improved immensely. The team knows exactly what’s happening in real-time, our drivers are kept in the loop, and we cut down on the back and forth explains Ryan . “ Our Eureka systems have taken pressure off the office and allowed us to focus on the next opportunity, rather than constantly firefighting Eureka’s MoveIT TMS is built to help transport operators manage, coordinate, and oversee every aspect of their road haulage operations. From incoming customer jobs to final delivery, MoveIT offers the visibility and tools needed to fulfil orders efficiently and reliably. Eureka has given us the visibility and structure that we need to be successful adds Ryan . “ Planners can see the bigger picture ahead of time, and our traffic coordinators can manage the day-to-day without chasing their tails. It has given us control and accountability. What used to take hours now takes minutes. Admin staff can focus on invoicing, compliance and customer service instead of just pushing paper Improving communication with drivers through Telegram The introduction of Telegram integration has been particularly valuable. Telegram, the instant messaging mobile app, integrates with Eureka MoveIT TMS, allowing office staff to seamlessly contact drivers with job information, delivery updates, and delivery documentation (such as PODs). It’s made communication with drivers seamless. Instead of phone calls and text messages, drivers get their job info straight away. Ryan describes just massive time-saving benefits provided through this integration, saving over 2.5 hours a day, just on driver communication. Before MoveIT and Telegram, we texted job details to our drivers, the day before the job. This used to take us 3 hours a day. Now, it’s more like 30minutes! The Telegram integration with Eureka also enables live translation, ensuring that communication and messages shared with drivers whose first language is not English are clearly understood. This helps to minimise disruptions and keep operations running smoothly. Improving customer relationships with Eureka With plans to grow the business, Ryan and the team needed a system that could help support their customer service with their existing clients. Part of this, was ensuring customers are informed with real-time, accurate information and updates, throughout their delivery journey. With improved communication directly with their drivers, the traffic team have full view of delivery progress across all of their jobs, providing them with the visibility they need to update customers. Now when a customer rings looking for an update, we can give it to them instantly, says Ryan . “ No waiting around, no chasing drivers for updates, just straight answers. That reliability is what keeps customers coming back Eureka’s dedicated and integrated Workshop module, MaintainIT, has also had a big impact on the team at Stateline. It’s been really useful explains Ryan . “ With so many trucks and trailers on the road, keeping on top of maintenance is a full time job, and MaintainIT keeps us ahead of the curve MaintainIT allows users to monitor vehicle maintenance schedules, log inspection checks, order new parts, and record vehicle work logs. With full integration with MoveIT, it adds a greater level of visibility for vehicle and trailer availability. A Partnership That Works For Stateline, the benefits of Eureka extend beyond the software. The relationship with the Eureka team has been consistently positive. We have a very good working relationship with the Eureka team. They are approachable, they listen, and they understand transport. When we need something, they’re quick to respond, and that support makes all the difference When asked what advice they would give to other transport businesses in Ireland considering Eureka, Ryan is clear: If you’re serious about scaling your business and cutting out the noise, Eureka is the way forward. It’s not just a system; it’s a way to professionalise how you run transport What the future holds for Stateline Logistics Group Despite rapid growth in recent years, Ryan and the team at Stateline Logistics have no plans to slow down. In addition to a new storage container depot scheduled to open in 2026, Ryan aims to expand their fleet to over 100 vehicles within the next five years, doubling its current size. With Eureka as a trusted partner, Stateline Logistics Group is confident it can achieve its growth ambitions and remain one of Ireland’s leading logistics providers. More about Stateline Logistics Group here https://stateline.ie/ More about Eureka here https://www.eureka.ie/ Eureka Contact 🗣️ Phil Newton 🏢 Head of Sales & CSM (Eureka) 📞 M: +44 (0) 7791 074 636 pnewton@eureka.ie Marketing Contact 🗣️ Jack Hall 🏢 Marketing (Mandata & Eureka) 📞 P: 0191 250 2220 marketing@eureka.ie Eureka is Ireland’s leading provider of transport management systems, with over 4,000 daily users of its software. The Co. Wexford-based company has been successfully developing TMS solutions for the transport and logistics industry for over 25 years. page_title: Mandata TMS Integration With Freeway Fleet Systems - Mandata
meta_description: Mandata is proud to integrate with leading fleet and garage management software, Freeway Fleet Systems. Read more about our integration here.

text: As a valued Mandata business partner, we work closely with Freeway to implement and support the Workshop system. In its most basic form, Freeway Workshops is a maintenance records system designed to ensure your vehicles and trailers are maintained to recommended service schedules. Full integration with the Mandata TMS and Mandata’s range of Add-ons ensures that a vehicle maintenance system can be operated in harmony with your TMS; optimising planning processes around scheduled vehicle maintenance time, to lower incidents of VOR, and monitoring vehicle performance alongside driver behaviour to maximise profits. Richard Bird, Mandata’s Commercial Director , says, “ Patrick and the Freeway team do an exceptional job in helping operators keep their fleets running smoothly and compliantly. By integrating Freeway with Mandata, our customers can plan more efficiently and manage their fleet maintenance seamlessly – an ideal solution for medium to large operators.” Patrick Tandy Managing Director at Freeway , adds, “ Our long-standing partnership with Mandata has been built on collaboration and shared goals. Together, we’ve helped transport operators achieve greater efficiency, compliance, and control. We’re proud of what we’ve accomplished so far, and excited for what’s ahead What the integration between Mandata and Freeway offers users: Improve vehicle utilisation: Integration with the Mandata TMS and Freeway Workshop software enables you to plan repairs and maintenance in line with your transport operations to improve planning, maximise revenue and reduce VOR incidents. Reduce vehicle off-road occurrences: Ensure your planners are aware of planned and ad hoc vehicle maintenance; bookings can be logged automatically within the TMS when integrated with the Freeway Workshop module. Any vehicles booked in the workshop for work are highlighted by a colour within the Mandata Traffic Pad to ensure vehicles are not planned in for jobs during that time. Manage vehicle maintenance efficiently: With maintenance planned around work schedules, you can minimise the impact on your business by having vehicles off-road. Produce detailed reports via your TMS: Export detailed reports on labour costs, defect types, vehicle warranties and much more. Log issues identified by drivers: Any issues identified during Vehicle Checks carried out on the driver’s PDA and mobile devices are automatically highlighted in Mandata Web Services Manager allowing for effective maintenance planning. Manage expenditure by vehicle: Control the financial aspects of vehicle maintenance with the Freeway Workshop module by highlighting items of high costs such as fuel or tyres, repairs and maintenance. What this means for transport operators: Live view of vehicle availability as you plan A smoother way to deliver on your transport promises Simplified workshop and vehicle management Better fuel, parts, and procurement tracking Improved compliance and operational efficiency The Mandata and Freeway teams both exhibited at the inaugural Road Transport Expo Scotland in September 2025, speaking to some of Scotland’s largest operators and discussing how both sets of software offer solutions to many of the industry’s challenges, faced day in and day out.

section: Google End of Life – Android OS 5.1.1
text: 31 October 23 Google has announced changes to security requirements which affect all Android applications. This mandate will impact your company’s ability to use Mandata apps in the future. To increase security, Google has raised the bar which dictates the level of the Software Development Kit (SDK) we use in our Android Applications. To be able to install Android apps on the latest version of Android (Android 14), we must now target the Google SDK #23 as a minimum. What this means in practice is that earlier versions of Android that do not support SDK #23, will not be able to receive app updates or re-install the app if it is uninstalled. What does this mean to your business? If you are using devices dating back to 2014 and run Android 5.1. you will be affected by this new Google mandate. We anticipate that you have at most 4-6 months to update devices running Android 5.1, however we strongly encourage you to consider updating them earlier, as this timeline is not guaranteed and is outside of Mandata’s control. Please contact your Mandata Customer Success Manager if you have any queries or concerns.

text: 28 November 23 Microsoft ended support for MS Windows 7 on 14 January 2020, and MS Windows 8.1 on 10 January 2023. This means PCs running either version of MS Windows will no longer be receiving essential security updates. After this date, the Mandata Web Services Manager application will no longer function on a PC running the MS Windows 7 or 8 Operating Systems. What Operating System does Mandata’s Web Services Manager Support? For the security of your own business, as well as for compatibility with our Web Services Manager application, you should use an Operating System that is supported by Microsoft. Currently Microsoft supports two Operating Systems: MS Windows 10 MS Windows 11 Mandata would recommend that you use MS Windows 11 and ensure that it is fully updated with the latest security patches from Microsoft. Please contact your Mandata Customer Success Manager if you have any queries or concerns.

section: Support ending for Stirling TMS – 1 June 24 (date extended to 31 December 24)
text: Effective 1 June 2024, the Stirling TMS product (a Mandata Group product) will be discontinued from both a user license and a support perspective. For more information, please see the FAQs below. Why is Mandata discontinuing Stirling TMS? When the businesses came together in 2021, there was an in-depth review of both product architecture and functionality. Following that analysis, a decision was made to retire the Stirling TMS product and for all future investment to be focused on a single platform; the Mandata TMS product. The Mandata technology had already seen significant modernisation investment in the previous few years and the decision was centred on ensuring best long term platform for all customers; a highly secure TMS that is scalable, configurable, is accessible anytime and has a clear future roadmap. Is Mandata discontinuing any of its own products? No, this relates solely to the Stirling TMS product. When does the End of Life take effect? Licensed usage and software support for the Stirling TMS product will come to an end 1st June 2024 (deadline extended in February 24 to 31 December 2024). Will I still have technical support for Stirling TMS after the EoL date? Support for customers will end on 1 June 2024 (deadline extended in February 24 to 31 December 2024). Are all of the Stirling products being discontinued No, the Stirling WMS is not and for more details on other Stirling apps please refer to this table. Why is Mandata providing advance notice? Mandata is providing advance notice to give customers sufficient time to prepare, research and decide on the best TMS for their business going forward. What solutions or products does Mandata recommend moving forward? Mandata’s TMS products (Go, GoPlus, Enterprise) are available to customers across a range of business sizes and requirements. In addition, key features from the Stirling TMS have been built into the Mandata TMS offer, to ensure continuity of functionality for Stirling customers, should they choose to migrate to a Mandata TMS product. What support is Mandata offering for migration to the Mandata product? Mandata has recruited additional implementation team members to ensure a smooth migration process that can be planned to minimise any disruption. We have streamlined the migration process, using experience of those Stirling customers who have already made the switch. We are offering rolling webinars on product demonstrations and have bolstered our customer teams to help during the transition. Where can I get more information? Customers that are currently using the Stirling TMS product will have received a call from either James Jarvis to ask any questions and discuss their options.

section: What Do You Need To Do Next?
text: Please find a replacement accounts package for your business. Although your current software will remain active until May 2026, we advise starting the process of selecting an alternative as soon as possible. Please ensure your new system is fully operational by 1st May 2026 to avoid disruptions to your accounting processes. Export and back up any necessary data from the Stirling Accounts system before the deadline to ensure a smooth transition, and to ensure you can access your complete historical records, if required. We recommend storing your archive Stirling Accounts system on a well-maintained PC/Server, as you may need to access the details up to 7 years after your financial year end. Please note that it is the responsibility of each customer to securely retain and maintain their own archived copy of the Stirling Accounts data after the system end-of-life date. Mandata will not be able to provide access, support, or data recovery once the system is no longer supported. Consider purchasing a Lifetime Archive Licence , allowing continued access to the system after 1st June 2026 for tasks such as running reports or reprinting invoices. Please click here to contact our team and request further details. Read FAQ

page_title: FAQ - Stirling Accounts - Mandata

text: Unfortunately, you will no longer be able to use Stirling Accounts once it is retired. You will only be able to access your historical records. We have notified HM Revenue & Customs that the system will no longer be available from 31 May 2026. Anyone continuing to use the software after this date risks non-compliance with Making Tax Digital requirements and may be unable to submit Tax and VAT Returns electronically. In addition, Mandata will be unable to provide support, updates, or security fixes beyond the retirement date. To access your data after 31st May 2026, please purchase a Lifetime Archive Licence. This will enable you to access the system for reporting and historical data retrieval. Please click here to complete the form below to request archive license details. The Lifetime Archive Licence allows you to retain long-term access to your existing accounts data. Please note that, once the software has reached End of Life (EOL), it will no longer supported or maintained by our development team. This means we will not be responsible for resolving any future issues that may arise due to: Changes or updates to your hardware, server, operating system, or virtual environment Windows or SQL Server updates, patches, or version upgrades Data corruption, loss, or compatibility issues with newer third-party software Network configuration changes or external system integrations failing Installation, migration, or reinstallation requirements following hardware failure or IT infrastructure changes In short, while you will retain indefinite access under this licence, any technical or environmental changes that render the software unusable will not be covered or supported by us. We therefore recommend maintaining appropriate system backups and documentation to preserve access for as long as technically feasible. With an Archive Licence, you will be able to access the system and operate this as you normally would. Mandata TMS customers: Your TMS can be integrated with your chosen accounts’ platform and we will help you with this process. All other functionalities will remain unchanged. Stirling WMS customers: We are releasing new functionality that enables core invoicing tasks to run independently of Stirling Accounts. This will require a system update, and we’ll contact you once it’s available. Integration with your chosen accounts’ platform will also be supported. Stirling Workshop customers: Similar to WMS, new functionality will allow core invoicing tasks to run without Stirling Accounts. A system update will be required, and we’ll notify you when it’s ready. Integration with your chosen accounts’ platform will also be supported. Unfortunately, no extensions will be available. The system will be decommissioned on the announced date. Unfortunately, we’re unable to advise or endorse specific accounts software vendors. If you are a Mandata TMS customer, we can advise whether the accounting software you choose will integrate with your transport management system and how it will support your processes. Mandata integrates with most major accounting providers. If you’d like further details, please get in touch with your Customer Success Manager or email: csm@mandata.co.uk As Stirling Accounts approaches its retirement, we’ll work with you to transition your hosted system from the Mandata Cloud to your own local environment. The data can be copied from the Cloud to your own device, provided the database is under 10GB in size. Please note that Stirling Accounts requires Microsoft SQL Express, which supports databases up to 10GB. If your data exceeds this limit, you’ll need to purchase a Microsoft SQL Server licence. Once transferred, your archived system will operate in a read-only mode, allowing you to view and reference historical information as needed. After the transition is complete, Mandata will no longer host Stirling Accounts in the Cloud environment. text: Avoid data input errors and duplication, save time and automate the transfer of information with Mandata. Details that need to be updated regularly can be automatically entered into your Mandata Enterprise TMS to save time and preserve data accuracy. Mandata integrations are highly efficient. Large data files can be transferred in minutes and a fraction of the time it would take you to input information manually. We currently integrate with over 10,000 customers’ systems. 70% of all work going through Mandata Enterprise TMS is automatically created for our customers, ready to plan, saving significant time and effort.
page_title: Integration Partners - Mandata

section: A Mandata TMS makes it easier for you.
text: Save time, avoid duplication and errors. All your customer details and resources managed in one place. Customer data is completed for you, as you create jobs. Job templates can be set up for repeat work, with standard jobs created in just a few clicks. Customers can create their own jobs in a customer portal, then they appear in your TMS ready to plan. Jobs can be imported from external sources automatically, saving you and your customers time and preserving data accuracy.
page_title: Easy Job Creation and Pricing for Hauliers l Mandata TMS
meta_description: Taking too long to create collections and deliveries? Let a transport management system take the heavy load. Request a demo today.

section: A TMS remembers your customer details, so you don't have to!
text: All important customer details such as names, addresses, key contacts, rates, site restrictions etc are saved in the TMS. When it comes to creating jobs, all this data can be completed automatically for you or recalled easily if you add adhoc work.
page_title: Easy Job Creation and Pricing for Hauliers l Mandata TMS
meta_description: Taking too long to create collections and deliveries? Let a transport management system take the heavy load. Request a demo today.

text: Mandata provide a variety of different methods to import jobs into the TMS, making it easy for you, and your customers. Popular formats such as CSV files and fixed length text files can be used. Or alternatively, it’s possible for us to integrate with your customer’s systems, to import orders automatically.
page_title: Easy Job Creation and Pricing for Hauliers l Mandata TMS
meta_description: Taking too long to create collections and deliveries? Let a transport management system take the heavy load. Request a demo today.

text: Customers log into the portal, input all the key data and the job will appear in your TMS, along with a notification that a new customer job has been received. Once underway, customers can see job progress and download PODs as each job is invoiced.

text: Whether you’d like to charge by mile, weight, number of pallets, timed deliveries, next day or a combination of different rates; the system can handle your requirements. text: Route optimisation software calculates the most efficient routes for haulage vehicles to complete collections and deliveries by considering factors like distance and delivery time windows. Using integrated TMS and route optimisation software hauliers can: reduce the miles they travel, lowering fuel costs, reducing empty miles, cO2 emissions. It saves time planning and refining the plan and customer service can be improved (by planning to time windows) as you can avoid overcommitting your fleet, you’re more likely to be on time. Route optimisation software is suitable for any haulier or own account operator, that wants to increase the efficiency of their fleet, but the greatest improvements will be seen by hauliers who handle multi-drop loads. No, we have partnered with Optimize, a specialist in AI Route Optimisation. The software is built into our software, which makes it easier for planners to use as they no longer have to go between multiple systems. Just click a button to optimise loads, see the results and make changes, if required.

section: Get live ETA progress and exception alerts
text: When stops are sent to the Navigation app via the Manifests app, the Navigation app will generate initial ETAs as soon as the driver starts navigation. These ETAs are sent by SMS or email to you, the relevant customers, and the contacts at collection and delivery sites. As the journey continues, the Navigation app continually re-evaluates each ETA. If an ETA changes by 5 minutes or more, a new SMS or email is sent, keeping everyone informed, in real time, of the driver’s progress.
page_title: Satnav and ETAs for HGV Transport Operators | Mandata
meta_description: Ensure drivers take the most safe and efficient routes with the ManifestPlus app. Our satnav app optimises routes, and provides real-time ETAs.

section: Get spoken directions in a language of your choice
text: Voice instructions, with turn-by-turn navigation, are available in most languages with text-to-speech and pre-recorded voice skins. Pre-recorded voice skins provide basic manoeuvre instructions such as “turn right in 200 metres”, while text-to-speech voices also provide spoken street names, for example, “turn right in 200 metres onto Northumberland Street”.
page_title: Satnav and ETAs for HGV Transport Operators | Mandata
meta_description: Ensure drivers take the most safe and efficient routes with the ManifestPlus app. Our satnav app optimises routes, and provides real-time ETAs.

section: Get the right route for each vehicle
text: Routes are optimised using the vehicle’s dimensions, number of trailers, and whether you want to avoid difficult turns and toll roads. These settings can be saved at fleet or vehicle level in the TMS and passed to the Navigation app to save time. The driver is prompted to check the settings before navigation begins and to change them if necessary, giving you full control of each journey’s specifics.
